perturbation.original_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduced or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreement; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party may retain copies (in whatever form reproduced or stored) of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreement; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'shall promptly return or destroy all copies' with 'may retain copies', the Receiving Party is no longer obligated to return or destroy confidential information, creating a legal contradiction because German law protects trade secrets and proprietary information, expecting reasonable measures to prevent unauthorized use or disclosure, including the return or destruction of such information upon termination of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: GeschGehG § 2, para 1 Nr. 1 (German Trade Secrets Act)
perturbation.law_explanation: § 2 defines trade secrets and the reasonable measures to keep the information secret. Allowing the Receiving Party to 'retain' copies contradicts the expected 'reasonable measures' to protect confidential information under German law.

perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the Receiving Party to 'shall promptly return or destroy all copies,' while the changed text allows the Receiving Party to 'may retain copies'. This directly contradicts the expectation of 'reasonable measures' to protect confidential information as described in the Law Explanation, which aligns with the perturbation explanation. The absence of scraped snippets does not negate the legal contradiction, as the explanation provided details German law expecting reasonable measures to prevent unauthorized use or disclosure, including the return or destruction of such information upon termination of the agreement. Allowing the retention of confidential information upon termination directly undermines the protection of trade secrets and proprietary information, thus creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information received, except to its em ployees, representatives or agents who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the confide ntial quality of the information provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement. Hence, the Receiving Party w ill be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information received, except to its employees, representatives or agents who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the confidential quality of the information provided.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement' and 'the Receiving Party will be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents.', the Receiving Party no longer explicitly needs to get an agreement from their employees and cannot be held fully liable for the actions made by their personnel. This weakens the confidentiality protection and introduces legal risk under German law, which requires establishing adequate internal measures to safeguard trade secrets.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 5 of the GeschGehG emphasizes the responsibility of the trade secret holder to take 'reasonable steps' to keep information secret. Omitting the explicit requirement to secure agreements from employees and removing the liability clause weakens these steps and potentially violates German trade secret law.

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates that the Receiving Party ensures their employees agree to keep confidential information on the same terms as the agreement and holds the Receiving Party fully liable for any breaches by their personnel. The changed text omits these requirements. The Law Explanation states that § 5 of the GeschGehG emphasizes the responsibility of the trade secret holder to take 'reasonable steps' to keep information secret and that omitting the explicit requirement to secure agreements from employees and removing the liability clause weakens these steps. The Perturbation Explanation states that this weakens the confidentiality protection and introduces legal risk under German law, which requires establishing adequate internal measures to safeguard trade secrets. Therefore, the omission of these critical obligations directly contradicts the need for reasonable steps to protect trade secrets.
